new_jobs.title: GIS and Environmental Specialist
new_jobs.description:
About ORCD
Organization for Research and Community Development (ORCD) is a national Afghan NGO registered in 2011. It has delivered more than 119 projects worth over USD 69 million across 31 provinces in health, nutrition, WASH, food security, agriculture, livelihoods, protection, and climate resilience. ORCD employs around 1,800 staff, at least 30% of them women, and is an implementing partner for nine UN agencies.

Job Descriptions
We are inviting CVs for the position of GIS and Environmental Specialist for an integrated livelihoods, forestry, and rangeland management program across Badakhshan and Kunar. The role will produce the spatial evidence base that guides site selection, intervention design, and progress verification. The specialist will carry out GPS-tagged mapping of intervention sites, prepare spatial layers for forestry, rangeland, and livelihood activities, and conduct environmental screening to flag risks and safeguard requirements. The role will support rapid ecological assessment, degradation mapping, and the production of clear maps and figures for technical packages and reports. The specialist will work closely with the field team to ground-truth data and to maintain a verifiable geographic record of all activities. Accuracy, strong data management, and the ability to turn field data into decision-ready products will be central to this position.

Job Requirements
Candidates must hold a minimum of a bachelor's degree in GIS, environmental science, or a related discipline. A minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in GIS mapping and environmental assessment is required. Applicants must show practical experience in spatial analysis, environmental screening, and site selection. Proficiency with standard GIS software, GPS data collection, and the production of maps and spatial datasets is expected. Experience supporting field teams in remote and difficult terrain is an advantage. Fluency in Pashto and Dari is required, and proficiency in oral and written English is required. Candidates should be precise, well organized, and able to deliver reliable spatial products on short timelines while maintaining clear documentation suitable for technical review and verification.

Submission Guidelines
We require multiple CVs for this position. Only candidates who meet more than 95% of the stated requirements should apply through this link: https://forms.gle/pq7DXu1W3p9q844N6
Your CV must contain all of the following:
Full name
Physical address
Email address
Education
Experience (month and year, and a job description for every position)
Languages
References (at least two)
Use this template for your CV: https://docs.google.com/document/d/16xM-b1PeKOD6N5j4yVy9M70RHp_OpNHR/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=110577270346576244457&rtpof=true&sd=true In the recognition of their efforts, applicants sending their CV in this format might be prioritized if they meet the qualifications.

Submission Email
https://forms.gle/pq7DXu1W3p9q844N6
Apply

details.Education: Not specified | Refer to Job Requirements

new_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Developer (Re Announced)
new_jobs.salary: NTA Higher grades as per experience relevancy!
new_jobs.description:
About Ministry of Finance
Directorate of Information Communication Technology at Ministry of Finance mainly focusing in the area providing data and voice services for MoF operations using highly-equipped data center, unified transmission system, and interconnected links via fiber and microwaves.
Directorate of ICT is providing reliable and secure platform for overall data and voice connectivity and access to MoF core application services in MoF HQs and sites.

Job Descriptions
The Senior Full Stack Developer will play a key role in leading and contributing to the design, development, and implementation of complex software systems and applications for the Directorate of ICT at the Ministry of Finance. The job requires strong skills in both front-end and back-end development, plus the ability to turn business needs into reliable, secure, and easy-to-use systems. The role also includes guiding the team and helping improve overall development practices.

Job Requirements
Key Responsibilities
The authorities concerned will carry out the following tasks and activities but not necessarily restricted to those:
Software Design & Development
Design, develop, test, and maintain enterprise-grade web applications using ASP.NET Core, C#, EF Core, and modern front-end frameworks (e.g., React).
Build and maintain RESTful APIs using .NET Core, applying CQRS and Microservices Architecture where appropriate.
Analyze, refactor, and enhance existing systems developed in C# .NET or PHP Laravel.
Apply Clean Architecture / Multi-layer Architecture, SOLID principles, and repository patterns.
Security, Quality & Performance
Implement secure coding practices in line with OWASP Top 10 and government ICT security standards.
Ensure unit test coverage of at least 80% And participate in integration and automated functional testing.
Conduct code reviews to ensure quality, maintainability, and compliance with international standards.
Identify performance bottlenecks and ensure high availability and reliability of applications and databases.
DevOps & Deployment
Create and maintain Docker configurations for containerized application deployment.
Support CI/CD pipelines and participate in release and change management processes.
Provide Swagger /OpenAPI documentation for all REST APIs.
Work with PostgreSQL and/or Microsoft SQL Server, ensuring data integrity and performance.
Agile & Collaboration
Actively participate in Agile/SCRUM ceremonies including sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives.
Use tools such as Git, GitHub, Jira, and collaboration platforms to support efficient delivery.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and other departments to achieve ICT objectives.
Leadership & Continuous Improvement
Mentor junior developers and promote best practices in software engineering.
Recommend and implement solutions to automate and simplify business processes.
Perform risk assessments, identify mitigation strategies, and ensure compliance with ICT governance policies.
Prepare and maintain technical documentation, operational manuals, and user guides.
Reporting & Administration
Perform any other ICT-related duties as assigned by management.
Provide regular progress updates, reports, and risk assessments to management as per SLA.
Eligibility and Skill Sets:
The candidate qualification is:
Bachelor’s or master degree in computer science, information technology and other related fields.
5 years for master and 7 years for bachelor of computer since graduates, information technology and other related fields with experience in the enterprise level software development.
Full understanding and proven experience of coding regarding enterprise application projects using object-oriented paradigm with the help of Microsoft .net core and Entity framework (EF) Core. The design pattern for code management will be used either multi-layer architecture or clean architecture/ clean coding.
Understanding of implementing API gateways and microservices.
Understanding and proven experience in design patterns specifically repository patterns and clean architecture implementation in C#.
Preferred experience in implementation complex application development concepts such as distributed cashing, dependency induction, retry and scheduling mechanism, background/log running processes.
Coding review, SOLID principles and OWASP top 10 Web Application Security knowledge would be considered as plus point.
ASP.NET, Java and React, RabbitMQ, Quartz.Net, JWT and OAuth knowledge will consider as plus points.
Knowledge of business processing simplification in enterprise environment, with open-source platforms, and enterprise software applications.
Experience in the development and implementation of standards, procedures and guidelines to support operational processes.
Self-motivated with the ability to prioritize, meet deadlines, and manage changing priorities;
Strong interpersonal, communication skills and Experience in technical writing such as functional specification, technical specification, etc.
Experience in quality assurance & mitigation/ control, risk analysis and data analysis.

Submission Guidelines
Qualified applicants are encouraged to submit their CVs with a detailed application letter and contact details, no later than 19-Jun-2026 to below e-mail:
Please clearly indicate “position name/vacancy number in the subject line.
Please do not attach your education and working experience documents.

Submission Email
nta@mof.gov.af
Apply

new_jobs.title: Senior Backend Java Developer
new_jobs.description:
Role Summary
The Senior Backend Java Developer at AurumPay is responsible for designing, building, and launching scalable backend services, FinTech solutions, and API integrations. This role connects AurumPay’s core systems with third-party services such as mobile operators, e-money providers, and digital wallets, focusing on secure, high-performance server-side logic.Key Responsibilities:

Duties and Responsibilities
1. Backend Development & Innovation
• Lead backend development for FinTech projects including digital wallets, payment engines, and automation tools.
• Propose and prototype new features to improve system performance and reliability.
• Ensure continuous development and enhancement of the existing AurumPay Mobile Money system backend.
2. Software Quality & Database Management
• Oversee backend development using Java, Spring Boot, and related libraries (Hibernate, Spring Security).
• Ensure all services meet security, performance, and compliance standards.
• Design and optimize Oracle databases; write complex SQL queries and manage database migrations.
3. API & Third-Party Integrations
• Build and support integrations using REST, SOAP, ISO 8583, and other industry-standard protocols.
• Integrate with mobile operators, digital wallets, EMIs, payment switches, and gateways.
4. System Design & Team Leadership
• Design scalable backend architectures and integration plans based on business needs.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to build resilient solutions.
• Provide technical guidance, code reviews, and mentorship to junior developers.
• Promote agile methodologies and CI/CD practices.

Job Requirements
Mandatory:
• Strong expertise in Java and Spring Boot (Spring MVC, Spring Data, Spring Security).
• Proven experience building REST/SOAP APIs and integrations using ISO 8583 and other industry protocols.
• Deep understanding of microservices architecture and full SDLC.
• Hands-on experience integrating with wallets, telecom operators, EMIs, or financial switches.
• Strong proficiency with Oracle databases (complex queries, indexing, performance tuning).
• Experience deploying applications on Oracle WebLogic Server or similar Java EE containers.
• Proficient with GitHub/GitLab and CI/CD pipelines.
• 5+ years of backend Java development experience in FinTech, payments, or technical integrations.
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field (Master’s is a plus).

Submission Guidelines
Interested candidates should submit their CV to following Email Address.
( jobs@aurumaf.com)

expiring_today.title: GIS and Environmental Specialist
expiring_today.company: Faradata Social Change and Women Empowerment Organization (FSCWEO)
expiring_today.description:
About Faradata Social Change and Women Empowerment Organization (FSCWEO)
Faradata Social Change and Women Empowerment Organization (FSCWEO) is a non-governmental, non-profit organization established on 20 July 2020 in Afghanistan. FSCWEO works to promote social change, empower women and youth, and support vulnerable communities through programs in education, health, WASH, livelihoods, human rights, and humanitarian assistance. With active offices in Kandahar, Herat, Helmand, Farah, Daikundi, Nangarhar and Nimruz, the organization aims to build an Afghanistan where all people—regardless of gender, ethnicity, or religion—live in dignity and equality.
Faradata Social Change and Women Empowerment Organization (FSCWEO) is a non-governmental, non-profit organization established on 20 July 2020 in Afghanistan. FSCWEO works to promote social change, empower women and youth, and support vulnerable communities through programs in education, health, WASH, livelihoods, human rights, and humanitarian assistance. With active offices in Kandahar, Herat, Helmand, Farah, Daikundi, Nangarhar and Nimruz, the organization aims to build an Afghanistan where all people—regardless of gender, ethnicity, or religion—live in dignity and equality.

Job Responsibilities
Job Responsibilities
Job Responsibilities

Conduct GIS-based spatial analysis to support environmental assessments, land-use planning, and site selection processes.
Prepare high-quality thematic maps, geospatial reports, and data visualizations for technical and management purposes.
Carry out environmental screening and assessments in accordance with project requirements and applicable environmental regulations.
Identify and analyze environmental risks and propose appropriate mitigation measures.
Support the selection of project sites through environmental and geospatial analysis.
Collect, verify, and manage spatial and environmental data from field surveys, satellite imagery, GPS devices, and other relevant sources.
Ensure the accuracy, quality, and integrity of GIS and environmental datasets.

expiring_soon.title: Data Science Trainer
expiring_soon.description:
Role Summary
NETLINKS is delivering a market-responsive digital skills program for Afghan adolescents and youth (ages 15–24) across Kabul and Herat. We are seeking an experienced Data Science Trainer to deliver Track B, which equips learners with the full "data-to-decision" pipeline and prepares them for real employment through the Jobs.af marketplace.
This is hands-on, applied teaching. Our model is "Applied Digital Mastery" — every concept is reinforced through real-world projects, not just lectures. You will work in small cohorts (low 25:1 student-to-instructor ratio) using a blended delivery format (two in-person sessions plus one online session weekly).

Duties and Responsibilities
Key Responsibilities
Deliver the Data Science & Information Management curriculum, covering data cleaning, SQL and database management, and building interactive dashboards using industry-standard visualization tools.
Teach the complete "data-to-decision" pipeline through live demonstrations, guided practice, and project-based assignments.
Design and grade practical projects that build a job-ready portfolio for each learner.
Conduct competency-based pre/post assessments and maintain accurate records in the Learning Management System (LMS).
Provide personalized mentorship to support learners at risk of falling behind, using LMS analytics to flag and intervene early.
Support learners' transition into internships, freelance work, and jobs, including CV workshops and onboarding to the Jobs.af Freelancer Marketplace.
Deliver content in a way that integrates with the program's mandatory life-skills module.
Maintain a safe, inclusive, and supportive learning environment at all times.
Comply fully with the project's safeguarding (SEA/SH), child protection, and data protection standards.

Job Requirements
Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Information Systems, or a related field.
Demonstrated practical proficiency in: data cleaning and preparation, SQL and relational database management, and data visualization / dashboard tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
Prior teaching, training, or facilitation experience, ideally with youth or adult learners.
Strong communication skills and the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to beginners.
Fluency in Dari and/or Pashto; working knowledge of English is an advantage.
Preferred / Desirable
Experience in a project-based or bootcamp-style teaching environment.
Familiarity with LMS platforms and blended (in-person + online) delivery.
Experience preparing learners for freelance or remote work opportunities.
Programming exposure (e.g., Python for data analysis) is a plus.
Important Notes
All staff must sign and adhere to a Code of Conduct, complete SEA/SH and safeguarding training before entering a classroom, and pass background checks.
Employment terms comply with Afghan labour law and international standards, including fair wages, reasonable working hours, and occupational health and safety provisions.

Submission Guidelines
Interested and qualified candidates are invited to submit through the Jobs.af ATS system under the designated vacancy section for this position.
Equal Opportunity and Safeguarding Statement
NETLINKS Ltd. is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, inclusion, and merit-based recruitment. Qualified candidates of both genders are encouraged to apply, and female candidates are highly encouraged to submit their applications.
NETLINKS maintains a zero-tolerance policy for sexual exploitation, abuse, and harassment (SEA/SH), child abuse, and any form of discrimination. The successful candidate will be required to sign and uphold the NETLINKS Code of Conduct, complete mandatory PSEA/SH and safeguarding training, and consent to background and reference checks.
Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

expiring_soon.title: Field Verification Officer
expiring_soon.location: Badakhshan Badghis Baghlan Balkh Bamian Daikondi Farah Faryab Ghazni Ghowr Helmand Herat Jawzjan Kabul Kandahar Kapisa Khost Kunar Kunduz Laghman Logar Nangarhar Nimruz Nuristan Oruzgan Paktia Panjshir Parwan Samangan Sar-e Pol Takhar Maidan Wardak Zabul Paktika
expiring_soon.salary: Compensation is assignment-based and will be discussed with shortlisted candidates.
expiring_soon.description:
About Verfied Digital Solutions
Verified Digital Solutions is a Canadian company specializing in accountability, transparency, and digital monitoring solutions for humanitarian and development programs. Verify is a program of Verified Digital Solutions, operating as an independent monitoring and verification platform that supports NGOs, donors, and implementing partners across Afghanistan and other complex environments. Through a combination of digital technology and trained field professionals, Verify confirms that reported activities, services, and aid distributions are delivered as planned and reach intended beneficiaries, strengthening accountability, improving data quality, and reducing the risk of misreporting, fraud, and aid diversion.
Website: verifieddigital.solutions

Job Description
Verify – a Canadian independent monitoring, verification, and transparency platform – is seeking qualified Field Verification Officers to conduct independent field monitoring of donor-funded and NGO-implemented projects across Afghanistan. Verification Officers will travel to project sites, engage with communities, collect evidence, and submit structured reports through Verify's secure digital system. This is a project-based consultancy; officers are assigned to missions based on geographic coverage and project needs.
Key Responsibilities:
Conduct field visits to project sites, including clinics, schools, food distribution points, infrastructure works, and community programs
Verify that reported activities, services, and distributions have taken place as planned
Interview beneficiaries, project staff, and community representatives using structured questionnaires
Collect geo-tagged photographs, GPS data, and monitoring forms using a smartphone
Submit accurate and timely reports through Verify's digital platform
Identify and report discrepancies, risks, or irregularities in project implementation
Maintain strict confidentiality, neutrality, and professional ethics at all times
Reporting and Supervision: Field Verification Officers work under the supervision of the Verify Monitoring Team. Assignments, tools, and reporting templates are provided through the Verify digital platform.
Compensation: Officers are compensated per completed and approved assignment. Payments are made via bank transfer following verification of submitted work.
Ethics and Compliance: All officers must comply with Verify's Code of Conduct, confidentiality policies, and data protection standards. Any falsification, misrepresentation, or conflict of interest will result in immediate contract termination.

Job Requirements
Job Requirements: All of the following requirements must be met:
Minimum three (3) years of professional experience in Monitoring and Evaluation, third-party monitoring, field verification, or humanitarian project monitoring
Fluency in English, Pashto, and Dari — spoken and written
Strong computer skills, including use of email, online forms, and digital reporting systems
Ability to use a smartphone for data collection, GPS tracking, and photo documentation
Ability to travel within assigned provinces and districts
High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail
Preferred: Experience working with NGOs, international organizations, or donor-funded programs is strongly preferred.

Submission Guideline
Interested candidates should submit the following to the email address below:
Full name
Current province and district
Phone number
Languages spoken
Summary of Monitoring and Evaluation experience
Updated CV / Resume
Two professional references (name, organization, and contact number)
Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Submission Email
careers@verifieddigital.solutions

expiring_soon.title: IT Officer
expiring_soon.salary: Salary and conditions are according to ACBAR’s salary scale.
expiring_soon.description:
About ACBAR
The Agency Coordinating Body for Afghan Relief and Development, ACBAR, is an independent forum founded in 1988 in response to the demand from NGOs working with Afghan refugees in Pakistan and across the border in Afghanistan to more efficiently coordinate assistance and delivery of services. With its main office in Kabul since 2002, ACBAR brings together 230 National and International NGOs working in in all sectors of humanitarian assistance and development in Afghanistan. Its activities focus heavily on information to its members and the aid community in general, coordination of activities at the national and regional levels, and advocacy on issues affecting the work of its members in Afghanistan. ACBAR has a mission to provide an efficient platform for members to carry out effective humanitarian, development, and peace building activities in Afghanistan. The members are the drive behind ACBAR, and their active participation is essential to ACBAR’s continued dynamism.

The IT Communication Officer is responsible for managing and delivering clear, effective communication between the IT department and internal/external stakeholders while supporting the organization’s IT systems. The role ensures efficient operation of hardware, software, and networks, translates technical information into user-friendly content, and supports users while safeguarding data and maintaining system reliability.

expiring_soon.title: Sharia Audit Officer
expiring_soon.description:
Role Summary
The Sharia Audit Officer is responsible for conducting periodic Sharia audits across the bank to ensure all products, services, contracts, transactions, and operational processes fully comply with Islamic Sharia principles and the rulings of the Sharia Supervisory Board. The role helps preserve the bank’s Islamic integrity and strengthens governance.

Duties and Responsibilities
1. Sharia Audit Planning & Execution
Participate in preparing the annual Sharia Audit Plan based on risk assessment.
Conduct field audits in branches and departments to verify adherence to Sharia guidelines.
Examine financing files, contracts, documentation, and transaction flows for Sharia compliance.
Test samples of Murabaha, Ijara, Musharaka, Mudaraba, and deposit transactions against Sharia standards.
2. Review of Contracts, Policies & Procedures
Audit the implementation of Sharia-approved product structures and documentation.
Ensure operational procedures match the product manuals approved by the Sharia Supervisory Board (SSB).
Check whether staff are following required Sharia workflows (e.g., asset ownership, agency structures, disclosures, penalties handling).
3. Identification of Sharia Non-Compliance
Identify Sharia violations, deviations, or gaps during audits.
Discuss findings with relevant departments and agree on corrective actions.
Ensure non-compliance events are recorded, reported, and resolved within the required timeframe.
4. Reporting & Documentation
Prepare clear Sharia audit reports with observations, recommendations, and compliance ratings.
Submit audit results to the Head of Sharia Audit and contribute to reports for the Sharia Supervisory Board.
Maintain proper documentation of all audit working papers and evidence.
5. Follow-Up & Corrective Actions
Follow up on previously reported Sharia findings to verify whether corrective actions were implemented.
Work with business units, risk management, operations, and compliance teams to close audit issues.
6. Coordination & Support
Coordinate with Internal Audit and Compliance Department for combined risk reviews.
Provide Sharia guidance to departments during audits to improve their understanding and performance.
7. Capacity Building & Awareness
Support the development of Sharia audit tools, checklists, and audit methodologies.
Assist in internal awareness sessions on Sharia compliance requirements for staff.
8. Continuous Improvement
Stay updated on AAOIFI Sharia standards, new fatwas, and industry best practices.
. Recommend improvements to Sharia audit processes and internal controls

Job Requirements
Qualifications & Experience
Bachelor’s degree in Islamic Studies, Sharia, Islamic Finance, Accounting, or Business Administration.
Professional certification in Islamic finance or Sharia audit (preferred).
1–3 years of experience in Islamic banking, Sharia audit, internal audit, or compliance roles.
Familiarity with AAOIFI standards and Islamic banking products.
Skills & Competencies
Strong understanding of Islamic banking and Sharia principles.
Ability to analyze financial transactions and documentation.
Good report writing skills with clarity and accuracy.
Strong attention to detail and analytical mindset.
Excellent communication and coordination abilities.
High integrity, objectivity, and professionalism.

Submission Guidelines
Applicants who meet the above requirements should submit their updated Curriculum vitae (CV)/Resume to (sr.recruitment-officer@ghazanfarbank.com)
Please Mention specific Job Title in subject of your E-mail otherwise your Email will not be considered.
Note: only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further recruitment process.

expiring_soon.title: GIS and Environmental Specialist
expiring_soon.description:
About ORCD
Organization for Research and Community Development (ORCD) is a national Afghan NGO registered in 2011. It has delivered more than 119 projects worth over USD 69 million across 31 provinces in health, nutrition, WASH, food security, agriculture, livelihoods, protection, and climate resilience. ORCD employs around 1,800 staff, at least 30% of them women, and is an implementing partner for nine UN agencies.

Job Descriptions
We are inviting CVs for the position of GIS and Environmental Specialist for an integrated livelihoods, forestry, and rangeland management program across Badakhshan and Kunar. The role will produce the spatial evidence base that guides site selection, intervention design, and progress verification. The specialist will carry out GPS-tagged mapping of intervention sites, prepare spatial layers for forestry, rangeland, and livelihood activities, and conduct environmental screening to flag risks and safeguard requirements. The role will support rapid ecological assessment, degradation mapping, and the production of clear maps and figures for technical packages and reports. The specialist will work closely with the field team to ground-truth data and to maintain a verifiable geographic record of all activities. Accuracy, strong data management, and the ability to turn field data into decision-ready products will be central to this position.

Job Requirements
Candidates must hold a minimum of a bachelor's degree in GIS, environmental science, or a related discipline. A minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in GIS mapping and environmental assessment is required. Applicants must show practical experience in spatial analysis, environmental screening, and site selection. Proficiency with standard GIS software, GPS data collection, and the production of maps and spatial datasets is expected. Experience supporting field teams in remote and difficult terrain is an advantage. Fluency in Pashto and Dari is required, and proficiency in oral and written English is required. Candidates should be precise, well organized, and able to deliver reliable spatial products on short timelines while maintaining clear documentation suitable for technical review and verification.

Submission Guidelines
We require multiple CVs for this position. Only candidates who meet more than 95% of the stated requirements should apply through this link: https://forms.gle/pq7DXu1W3p9q844N6
Your CV must contain all of the following:
Full name
Physical address
Email address
Education
Experience (month and year, and a job description for every position)
Languages
References (at least two)
Use this template for your CV: https://docs.google.com/document/d/16xM-b1PeKOD6N5j4yVy9M70RHp_OpNHR/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=110577270346576244457&rtpof=true&sd=true In the recognition of their efforts, applicants sending their CV in this format might be prioritized if they meet the qualifications.

Submission Email
https://forms.gle/pq7DXu1W3p9q844N6
Apply

tech_banking_jobs.title: Digital Banking Operation Manager (Re-announced)
tech_banking_jobs.description:
Role Summary
The Digital Banking Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing the daily operational activities and ensuring the smooth functioning of key units within the Digital Banking Department. This includes Card Issuance and Management, Settlement, Refund & Dispute Resolution, Customer Service and Support, and Business & Operations. The role ensures efficient service delivery, operational excellence, compliance with regulatory requirements, and enhanced customer experience across all digital banking services.

Duties and Responsibilities
Card Issuance and Management:
Supervise the end-to-end card issuance process including debit, credit, prepaid and instant AfPay, MasterCards & VISA.
Coordinate with vendors and internal stakeholders for timely card production, delivery, and stock management.
Ensure compliance with card security standards and central bank regulations.
Settlement, Refund & Dispute Resolution:
Oversee daily transaction settlement and reconciliation processes with networks such as MasterCard, Visa, and AfPay.
Manage refund processing and timely resolution of customer disputes and chargebacks.
Coordinate with Finance, IT, and external partners to ensure settlement accuracy and integrity.
Customer Service and Support:
Lead the digital banking support team to provide timely and effective responses to customer inquiries and issues.
Monitor service levels and continuously improve customer support processes.
Handle escalated cases and ensure a professional and solution-oriented response.
Coordinate with the marketing to effectively and efficiently advertise the digital banking products and services to attract customers and share accurate information about the AUB digital banking products and services in social medias and website.
Leads the call center team for timely support for the digital banking services and products.
Business and Operations:
Coordinate daily business operations of the Digital Banking Department.
Implement and monitor SOPs for all operational activities.
Liaise with internal departments (IT, Risk, Compliance, Finance, etc.) for issue resolution and service enhancements.
Track KPIs and generate performance reports for senior management.
Lead accruals booking and manage digital banking operation cost and expenses.
Cross-functional Responsibilities:
Work closely with the Digital Banking Technical Manager and Deputy Head of DBD to align operations with technical developments and strategic initiatives.
Support the rollout of new digital products and services.
Ensure readiness for audits, regulatory reviews, and compliance reporting.
Reporting and Documentation:
Maintain detailed technical documentation, architecture designs, and process flows for all managed systems.
Provide regular performance, risk, and incident reports to the Deputy Head and Head of DBD.

Job Requirements
Education:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Administration, Finance or related field (Master’s preferred).
Experience:
Minimum of 5 years of experience in digital banking systems, with at least 2 years in a technical leadership role.
Technical Competencies:
Strong understanding of digital banking services, card operations, and payment systems.
Experience in settlement and dispute management.
Excellent problem-solving, leadership, and communication skills.
Familiarity with core banking systems and digital platforms.
Fluency in English and local languages (Dari/Pashto) is an advantage.
Knowledge of Core Banking Systems (CBS).
Other Skills:
Strong leadership and problem-solving abilities.
Excellent communication and coordination skills.
Fluency in English; knowledge of Dari and Pashto is an advantage.

Submission Guidelines
Interested candidates who deem themselves suitable for this vacancy can share their complete profile including (application letter, updated CV, and recent photo) electronically to below mentioned email and please indicate the vacancy number AUB/EA/VA/026-018 in the subject line of your email otherwise your application will not be considered.

tech_banking_jobs.title: IT Solaris System Administrator
tech_banking_jobs.salary: As per the Bank Salary Scale
tech_banking_jobs.description:
About FMFB-A
About FMFB-A:
As a background, FMFB-A started its operation in 2004, and is part of the Aga Khan Agency for Microfinance (AKAM), which has financial institutions operating in different countries throughout the world. It is affiliated with the Aga Khan Development Network (AKDN), a group of development agencies working in health, education, culture and rural economic development. For more information, please visit our website: www.fmfb.com.af .

Job Descriptions
Job Summary:
The Oracle Solaris System Administrator will be responsible for setting up servers, installing servers, creating and implementing Linux configurations, networking servers, and troubleshooting.
The Oracle Solaris System Administrator looks after design, installation, maintenance, and retirement of the systems, application server, WebLogic Server, data, security of the systems that are at the core of the organization, This role is responsible for the Technical and server Administration for infrastructure: Designing, building and implementing IT infrastructure; Proving timely and high-quality completion of projects (Tasks and request and ensuring the IT infrastructure environment is up to data through proactive auditing and Patch management.
Position Duties and Responsibilities:
Install, configure, upgrade, maintain, and troubleshoot Solaris, Linux, and Windows servers, operating systems, applications, and related infrastructure.
Manage Production (PR) and Disaster Recovery (DR) environments, including load balancing, server migration, resource sharing, high availability, redundancy, backups, and business continuity solutions.
Administer user accounts, Active Directory, storage systems (SAN, NAS, Oracle ZFS), virtual machines, and data center infrastructure.
Monitor system performance, availability, security, backups, patches, upgrades, and disaster recovery readiness to ensure maximum uptime and operational efficiency.
Provide technical support, automate routine administration tasks, maintain documentation, and manage incident and outage reporting.
Administer Oracle FCCM, Oracle OBDX, Oracle WebLogic Servers, Smart Banking systems, and other enterprise applications.
Collaborate with IT Security, Network, and vendor teams to maintain infrastructure integrity, security, compliance, and performance.
Monitor data center operations, maintain standards and procedures, and recommend infrastructure improvements and technology upgrades.
Data Center Responsibilities:
Oversee daily health monitoring and reporting of core data center infrastructure.
Maintain documentation and event logs for processes and activities.
Provide recommendations for procuring new data center equipment as needed.
Keeping track of developing trends in data center technologies.
Collaborating with IT Security and network teams on cybersecurity and network efficiency.
Make sure the Data Center standard procedure followed by the team, units and vendor
efficiently.

Job Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
Minimum 5 years of experience in Solaris, Linux, Unix, and Windows system administration within enterprise or data center environments.
Strong knowledge of Active Directory, PowerShell scripting, virtualization, storage technologies, server hardware, operating systems, networking, security, and disaster recovery.
Excellent communication, documentation, reporting, and problem-solving skills.

Submission Guidelines
If you meet the above requirements, we are encouraging you to apply for this position. Please send us your CV along with a cover letter via email at ( vacancies@fmfb.com.af ).
Please make sure that your email includes the vacancy ID as the subject line.
Note that, only the candidates who are meeting the requirements of the position will be shortlisted.
Form more information, please vist the FMFB-A's website: Website

Submission Email
vacancies@fmfb.com.af
Apply

tech_banking_jobs.description:
Role Summary
Conducting field research in Baghlan Province, Afghanistan, or writing research reports on Afghanistan-related topics.
Requires quick work execution, competent English skills, honesty and reliability, and extensive knowledge or connections in Baghlan Province.

Duties and Responsibilities
Conducting field research in Baghlan Province, Afghanistan, or writing research reports on Afghanistan-related topics.

Job Requirements
Residing in Baghlan Province, Afghanistan; possessing basic English writing and communication skills; preference will be given to candidates with relevant government, military, or investigator experience; male candidates preferred

Submission Guidelines
Please submit your English resume on this website. Name your resume with your name and occupation. The resume should include your phone number, education, and detailed work experience (specifying position, company name, and responsibilities). Please update your resume regularly. Company staff will contact you via email; please check your inbox.
